title: Common Bug Patterns / Examples in C++

content.problemStatement:
In this lesson, you'll learn about common bug patterns that can appear while programming in C++. 
- Identifying these patterns will help you debug your code effectively and enhance your programming skills.
- As you tackle debugging, remember to keep your code organized using comments, and reading and understanding error messages can also guide you in identifying the source of a bug.

---

**Common Bug Patterns:**
1. **Off-by-One Errors in Loops**
```cpp
 // Off-by-One Error in Loops
    // Incorrect: Runs 6 times instead of 5 due to `i  0) {  // Correct conditional structure
        cout << "Positive";  // Missing closing brace may cause compilation errors
    } // Fix: Ensure proper closing brace for correct program flow

    return 0;  // Ensures clean program termination
}
```
